conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, could you please tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does Alex live with you, and do you provide for his care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me at home and I look after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to understand a bit more about Alex's circumstances.

Is Alex currently in full-time education or training? For example, is he attending school or college?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's in school. He's 15, so he's still in secondary school doing his GCSEs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about your immigration status and right to be in the UK.

Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status or another immigration status that gives you the right to live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen and I live here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? For example, another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question: Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £60,000 per year? 

This is important because if either you or your partner earns over £50,000, it can affect Child Benefit through the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim it.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you and you're responsible for his care
- You're a UK citizen with the right to claim
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He's in full-time education (secondary school)
- Your household income is under the threshold that would trigger any tax charges

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. You would typically receive:
- £25.60 per week for your eldest or only child (Alex)

Since neither you nor your partner earns over £50,000, you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit Charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (currently 15); 2) He lives with the claimant who is responsible for him; 3) The claimant is a UK citizen living in England and has the right to claim; 4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; 5) Alex is in full-time education (secondary school doing GCSEs), which is required at his age. Since the claimant and their partner earn under £60,000, there would be no High Income Child Benefit Charge to consider.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 15-year-old son Alex. All eligibility requirements are met: the child is under 16, lives with and is cared for by the claimant, the claimant is a UK citizen with the right to claim, no one else is claiming for the child, and the child is in appropriate full-time education. There are no income concerns that would trigger the High Income Child Benefit Charge.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (currently 15); 2) He lives with the claimant who is responsible for him; 3) The claimant is a UK citizen living in England and has the right to claim; 4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; 5) Alex is in full-time education (secondary school doing GCSEs), which is required at his age. Since the claimant and their partner earn under £60,000, there would be no High Income Child Benefit Charge to consider.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 15-year-old son Alex. All eligibility requirements are met: the child is under 16, lives with and is cared for by the claimant, the claimant is a UK citizen with the right to claim, no one else is claiming for the child, and the child is in appropriate full-time education. There are no income concerns that would trigger the High Income Child Benefit Charge.
